status.caveat: Torch profiler and trace export are highly intrusive. Step throughput is not promotion evidence; use this run for attribution only.

trace_selection.reason_iteration2_ignored: Torch profiler emitted a shutdown trace at step 2 and overwrote the real step-2 files with tiny empty traces. The summary artifact therefore uses step-1 traces plus step-2 sidecars.

additive_step2_wall_s.read: The profiler_step export is outside the train_step wall but contaminates log-to-log throughput; do not compare this run's tps to no-profiler baselines.

read: The active 12-layer probe is still dominated by the backward plus wrapper path: step-2 backward is 34.765s and the fwd/bwd wrapper residual is 11.339s.
MORI/MoE remains the largest named branch: step-2 rank-median local expert-loop owner VJP is 12.013s, with combine_dx at 2.011s median and 3.237s rank max.

The named MORI plus loss sidecars do not explain the full backward wall; after local VJP, dX/top-k return, grad dispatch, and loss, roughly 15-18s of backward remains in dense-fallback attention/projection/FSDP/autograd work.

The step-1 trace also shows large cumulative copy and RCCL/NCCL kernel time plus 7.817s median unattributed GEMM, so the next split should target FSDP/autograd synchronization and dense-fallback backward owners, not only MORI collectives.
Loss/lm-head is visible but secondary at about 1.48s per step. It should stay on the ladder, behind fused owner-rank MoE backward and wrapper/autograd/FSDP attribution.
next_actions: Replace Python per-expert owner VJP with compact/AITER-owned fused grouped training backward while preserving AITER fused forward.
Instrument or split the remaining backward-minus-MORI wall into dense-fallback attention, projection GEMM, FSDP all-gather/reduce, and autograd allocator/free buckets.
Keep no-profiler materialized throughput runs separate from torch-profiler runs; report token throughput only from low-intrusion or no-profiler candidates.
